title: Visualizing "We the People": Bridging the Perception Gap through Pluralistic Data Storytelling
abstract: arXiv:2606.24635v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Traditional visual data storytelling relies on binary graphics that depict two simplified groups in conflict. This can increase political polarization by oversimplifying intra-group disagreements and erasing ambiguity and shared ideas or values. This can inadvertently foster "us versus them" thinking. Intentional, pluralistic design choices for AI-enabled digital platforms can produce visualizations that emphasize nuance, opinion distribution, and intergroup commonalities. To demonstrate this potential, we examine deliberative technologies that map high-dimensional opinion spaces and highlight areas of both consensus and dissensus. The paper highlights the We the People deliberation conducted by Jigsaw and the Napolitan Institute in September 2025, which engaged over 2,400 Americans across all 435 congressional districts in an AI-supported, asynchronous dialogue regarding freedom and equality. translated_title: 可视化“我们人民”：通过多元数据叙事弥合认知鸿沟
translated_abstract: 传统视觉数据叙事依赖于描绘两个简化群体冲突的二元图形。这种叙事方式可能通过过度简化群体内部的分歧、抹去模糊性以及共享的思想或价值观，从而加剧政治极化。这可能在无意中助长“我们vs他们”的思维模式。针对人工智能驱动的数字平台，有意识地采用多元化的设计选择，可以产生强调细微差别、意见分布和群体间共同点的可视化结果。为了展示这种潜力，我们研究了能够映射高维意见空间并突出共识与分歧区域的审议技术。本文重点介绍了Jigsaw和Napolitan研究所于2025年9月开展的“我们人民”审议活动，该活动在全美435个国会选区中，让超过2400名美国人参与了一场关于自由与平等的、由人工智能支持的异步对话。通过利用人工智能来综合长篇幅讨论内容……
tldr: 本文提出通过多元化数据叙事设计，利用AI支持的大规模审议活动（如“我们人民”），以可视化方式展现意见分布和群体间共同点，从而弥合政治认知鸿沟、减少极化。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes pluralistic data storytelling design, leveraging AI-supported large-scale deliberation (e.g., "We the People") to visualize opinion distributions and intergroup commonalities, thereby bridging perception gaps and reducing political polarization.
